reasoning:
The Nominal Broad US Dollar Index (DTWEXBGS) has been on a protracted downward trend over the past year, dropping from ~127 in March 2025 to ~117.8 in late February 2026. This decline aligns with market analyst forecasts, which predominantly anticipate a weaker dollar in early 2026 as the Federal Reserve cuts interest rates (projected to reach 3%). 

The internal FRED linear model estimates a modest probability of ~0.30 for an increase over a short horizon (Mar 8), given the recent negative slope (-0.036/day). However, the combined FRED tool estimate, incorporating minor seasonal/prior-year effects, puts the probability at 0.48. Foreign exchange markets often exhibit random-walk behavior over short periods, which pushes probabilities near 0.50. Factoring in the recent bearish sentiment and downward momentum, I assign a slightly below-even probability for the March 8th resolution date (45%).

Following the instructions that uncertainty should increase with the forecast horizon and probabilities should revert closer to 0.50 for distant dates, I set the probability for the final resolution date on March 31st slightly closer to 50% (48%).
